vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1762: Swarm v2 — tracer-bullet planning contract (Path A) + foreman-driven intra-issue swarms (Path B)

plan.title: Swarm v2: tracer-bullet planning contract (Path A) + foreman-driven intra-issue swarms (Path B)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

references.label: PAN-1791 (downstream consumer, blocked on Path B)

narratives.Problem: PAN-1517 deleted the slot swarm (~6,021 lines); the in-context subagent replacement covers cheap mechanical parallelism but lost durable/registered workers, cross-machine distribution, and continuous integration with synthesis at DAG convergence. Single-issue wall-clock parallelism is gone just as big issues arrive, and PAN-1791 (a flagship) is blocked because it is designed to extend a foreman that does not exist. The deeper gap Directive exposed was never supervision — it was the CONTRACT: vertical tracer-bullet slices, declared file scopes + confidence, per-item verify commands, and a machine readiness audit before any worker launches. The old swarm had none of these; its slot merges into the feature branch ran zero quality gates.
narratives.Proposal: Two complementary paths, shipped together under one issue. Path A (planning discipline): mandate vertical tracer-bullet slicing in the canonical planning prompt; add typed contract metadata (files_scope + confidence, verify_commands, expected_outputs, tri-state readiness); extend the EXISTING finalize quality-lint with a readiness audit; record scope drift at pan done; surface cross-issue predicted-conflict signals. A single shared module (swarm-readiness.ts) computes swarmability ONCE and is consumed by both the lint and the foreman — swarmability is a property of the plan, not a special case. Path B (foreman): the issue's durable work agent drives waves itself via groupItemsByWave + hasFileOverlap (no server runtime, no route, no poller); dispatches each item via one of two tiers (in-context subagent vs registered slot agent rebuilt on spawnRun in per-item worktrees on feature/<issue>/slot-<n>); verify-then-merges each slot into the feature branch; composes in-context synthesis digests at convergence. Overlap is an ordering signal, never a refusal — the merge train (PAN-1691/PAN-1737) is the hard integration guarantee. Reuse what survives (dag.ts, spawnRun, harness subagents, resource-discovery); rebuild what PAN-1517 removed (per-item slot spawning, the wave-driver, verify-then-merge).
narratives.NonGoals:
- Restoring the old swarm runtime wholesale: routes/swarm.ts, the auto-advance poller, the slot-merged HTTP callback, SwarmRuntime/SwarmSlotRuntime/SynthesisOutput continue-state, the detectMergedSwarmSlots deacon patrol, SwarmDispatchDialog, and DEFAULT_SWARM_MODEL stay deleted (PAN-1517).
- Hard refuse-to-dispatch on predicted overlap at any level — overlap orders work, it never blocks; the merge train (PAN-1691/PAN-1737) is the guarantee (§1.4, §10.9).
- Per-slot PRs or per-slot review — the issue's single PR remains the review unit; slots are implementation steps reviewed together.
- Per-worker provider/model policy UI — Cloister routing already covers model choice.
- Cohort ceremonies (swarm:complete-cohort etc.) — issue close-out already owns lifecycle completion.
- Phase 6 remote slots on Fly — out of scope, tracked separately as PAN-1773.
- §10.6 boundary-map mechanical checks at wave convergence — deferred follow-up (the PRD marks them 'may optionally').
- §10.7 automated `pan plan split --check/apply` tooling — deferred follow-up; only the prompt-level 'prefer splitting' guidance ships in A1.
- §10.8 historical drift-table→confidence-default feedback loop — deferred follow-up; A4 ships drift RECORDING only.
- A new --strict finalize flag — not added; new lint checks are warn-first (errors only for malformed declarations), and --no-quality-lint is the existing emergency bypass.
- Special-casing PAN-1791 (or any issue id) in the foreman or dispatch — the dispatch decision is a pure function of item metadata.
- Refreshing or removing stale docs/SWARM.md + docs/swarm-diagrams/ — tracked separately as PAN-1555, not this issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Swarmability computed once in a shared module (swarm-readiness.ts) consumed by both the finalize lint and the foreman.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator ask #2: swarmability is a property of the plan, not a special case. One code path prevents the lint and the foreman from disagreeing about whether a plan is swarm-eligible.

autoDecisions.summary: Add optional TYPED metadata fields (files_scope_confidence, verify_commands, expected_outputs, readiness) instead of riding only the [key:string]:unknown index signature.
autoDecisions.rationale: Minor deviation from PRD §A1 'no schema change'; additive and backward-compatible; gives the lint and foreman type-safe consumption instead of `as` casts. files_scope already exists at types.ts:71.

autoDecisions.summary: Lint severity split: malformed declarations (broad globs §10.1, readiness:ready+confidence:low §10.2) are errors from the start; advisory checks (missing scope, overlap audit, large+parallel-safe) are warn-first. No new --strict flag.
autoDecisions.rationale: Reconciles §A2 'warn initially' with §10.1/§10.2 'reject'. A self-evidently malformed scope declaration is a typo-class error now; a prediction is advisory until scopes are reliably populated. --no-quality-lint already covers emergency bypass.

autoDecisions.summary: Addendum §10 split: ship §10.1–§10.5; defer §10.6 (boundary maps), §10.7 (automated split tooling), §10.8 (historical confidence feedback) to follow-ups.
autoDecisions.rationale: §10.6 is 'may optionally' per the PRD; §10.7/§10.8 are sizable refinements layered on top of the v1 core. The integral contract + analyzer + foreman ship; the prompt-level prefer-splitting guidance (A1) and drift recording (A4) still ship.

autoDecisions.summary: A3 cross-issue signals land as issue-footprint + predicted-conflicts; the 'launch-ordering soft preference' is the surfaced predicted-conflict signal, not a new hard scheduler.
autoDecisions.rationale: Faithful to §A3 'soft preference, not a gate' and reuses the existing flywheel-merge-order pairwise math; avoids inventing a scheduler the flywheel (an orchestrator agent) does not need.

items.title: Add typed swarm-contract metadata fields to VBriefItemMetadata

narrative.Action: Add optional typed swarm-contract fields to VBriefItemMetadata in src/lib/vbrief/types.ts (alongside the existing files_scope at :71 and difficulty at :67): files_scope_confidence?: 'high'|'medium'|'low'; verify_commands?: string[]; expected_outputs?: string[]; readiness?: 'ready'|'sequential'|'needs_refinement'. Keep the existing [key:string]:unknown index signature (:74). Export shared union types (e.g. FilesScopeConfidence, ItemReadiness) so the analyzer, lint, and foreman import them rather than re-declaring string literals. This is the cross-cutting schema every other bead encodes assumptions about.

items.title: Given the edited types.ts, when tsc runs, then VBriefItemMetadata exposes optional files_scope_confidence, verify_commands, expected_outputs, and readiness fields and the project typechecks with no errors

Given an existing spec that declares none of the new fields, when it is loaded, then it still typechecks and finalizes (the change is additive — no field is made required and it preserves backward compatibility)
Then types.ts exports shared union types for files_scope_confidence and readiness, and a unit reference importing them compiles
Mandate tracer-bullet slicing + contract metadata in the planning prompt

narrative.Action: Extend the canonical planning prompt src/lib/cloister/prompts/planning.md (NOT roles/plan.md) to mandate the swarm contract, added near the existing difficulty (:210) and foundationFor (:250) mandates in the same established style. Cover: vertical tracer-bullet slices; per-item files_scope + files_scope_confidence; verify_commands + expected_outputs for slot-eligible items; the tri-state readiness vocabulary (§10.3); and a 'prefer splitting big issues into independently shippable sibling issues' instruction (§A1, §10.7 prompt-level guidance only).

items.title: Then planning.md instructs planners to author items as vertical tracer-bullet slices (each independently verifiable end-to-end) and rejects horizontal layer-slices such as 'all the DB work' or 'all the UI work'
Then planning.md requires every item to declare metadata.files_scope plus files_scope_confidence, and to declare verify_commands and expected_outputs for slot-eligible items
Then planning.md documents the tri-state metadata.readiness (ready|sequential|needs_refinement) and when a planner applies each value

Then planning.md instructs planners to prefer splitting a big issue into independently shippable sibling issues over one mega-vBRIEF, stating siblings parallelize through the flywheel with full per-unit review/test/UAT
Mirror contract metadata fields into the write-vbrief skill

narrative.Action: Mirror the new contract metadata fields into sync-sources/skills/write-vbrief/SKILL.md so self-planning and work agents that author a spec directly emit files_scope, files_scope_confidence, verify_commands, expected_outputs, and readiness with the same names/values as types.ts and planning.md.

items.title: Then write-vbrief SKILL.md documents files_scope, files_scope_confidence, verify_commands, expected_outputs, and readiness using the same field names and allowed values as types.ts
Then the SKILL's example vBRIEF item contains the new fields populated with realistic values
When npm run lint runs, then the skills lint passes (no CLI/skill drift introduced)
Shared swarm-readiness analyzer (the single swarmability decider)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/vbrief/swarm-readiness.ts exporting a PURE analyzeSwarmReadiness(doc, opts?: { hotspots?: string[] }) that reuses dag.ts primitives (groupItemsByWave :39, hasFileOverlap :336, getDispatchableItems :217). Return a structured verdict: per item { id, readiness, slotEligible, scopeConfidence, missingScope, overlaps: { itemId, sharedFiles }[] } and plan-level { waves, conflictGroups, overlapMatrix, swarmEligible }. Files matched by opts.hotspots are excluded from ALL overlap math. Items with files_scope_confidence:'low' are treated as overlapping-with-everything for scheduling (conservative) but are never marked blocked. This module is the single swarmability decision consumed by both the A2 lint and the Path B foreman (operator ask #2).

items.title: Given a VBriefDocument, when analyzeSwarmReadiness runs, then it returns a plan-level verdict containing waves (from groupItemsByWave), an overlap matrix, conflict groups, and a swarmEligible boolean
Given two items with overlapping files_scope and no connecting blocks-edge, then the verdict marks them overlapping and lists the shared files
Given a hotspots list, when two items share only a hotspot file, then the verdict reports them non-overlapping (hotspot files excluded from overlap math)
Given an item with files_scope_confidence:'low', then the verdict treats it as overlapping-with-all for scheduling yet never sets its blocked status solely on that basis
Then analyzeSwarmReadiness is pure (performs no I/O and holds no process state) and is exported for reuse by the lint and the foreman
Per-project hotspot list excluded from overlap math

narrative.Action: Add an optional per-project hotspot list to the projects.yaml project config (e.g. a swarm.hotspots: string[] — CHANGELOG.md, bun.lock, generated files) and parse it in the project-config loader (src/lib/config-yaml.ts, and/or src/lib/projects.ts — confirm the exact ProjectConfig type at implementation). Plumb the resolved hotspots into analyzeSwarmReadiness({ hotspots }) at its call sites. Default to an empty list when unset. Hotspots are EXCLUDED from overlap math (complementary to broad-glob rejection, which rejects at declaration time).

items.title: Given a project that declares a swarm hotspot list, when the config loads, then the parser returns those hotspot patterns; when a project declares none, then it defaults to an empty list
When the analyzer is invoked for a project with hotspots, then those hotspots are passed through so hotspot files are excluded from overlap detection
Then a unit test covers a project with hotspots resolving to the declared list and a project without hotspots resolving to []
Per-item scope/field checks in the finalize quality-lint

expected_outputs: quality-lint.test.ts covers the four new per-item checks and passes

narrative.Action: Add per-item checks to lintItem in src/lib/vbrief/quality-lint.ts: (1) WARN when an item is missing files_scope; (2) ERROR on broad-glob scopes (src/**, bare directory names, **-suffixed roots) — these are malformed declarations, rejected from the start (§10.1); (3) cross-field consistency (§10.2): readiness:'ready' + files_scope_confidence:'low' → ERROR; difficulty in {complex,expert} marked parallel-safe with no stated reason → WARN; (4) expected_outputs entries reuse the existing VAGUE_AC_PATTERNS / BANNED_AC_PATTERNS (:24,:27) banned-phrase check (§10.4). Severity split per decision D7/§10.1/§10.2: malformed declarations are errors now, advisory checks are warn-first.

items.title: Given an item missing files_scope, when the lint runs, then it emits a warn-severity issue naming the item
Given an item whose files_scope contains a broad glob (src/**, a bare directory, or a **-suffixed root), then the lint emits an error-severity issue that blocks finalize
Given an item with readiness:'ready' and files_scope_confidence:'low', then the lint emits an error; given a complex|expert item marked parallel-safe with no stated reason, then it emits a warn
Given an expected_outputs entry containing a banned vague phrase (reusing VAGUE_AC_PATTERNS/BANNED_AC_PATTERNS), when the lint runs, then it emits a quality issue for that entry
Cross-item overlap audit (warn-only) in the finalize quality-lint

expected_outputs: overlap-audit test asserts warn-only severity and shared-file listing

narrative.Action: Add a cross-item overlap audit to lintPlanQuality (in src/lib/vbrief/quality-lint.ts) that calls analyzeSwarmReadiness and, for any two edge-unconnected sibling items with overlapping scopes (hotspots excluded), emits a WARN listing the shared files and advising the planner to add an edge, merge the items, or accept serialization. It MUST NEVER emit error and MUST NEVER refuse finalize — overlap is an ordering signal (§10.9). Lands in the same file as lint-item-checks (serialized after it).

items.title: Given two items with overlapping files_scope and no connecting blocks-edge, when the lint runs, then it emits a warn-severity issue listing the shared file(s)
Given the same two items connected by a blocks-edge, then the lint emits no overlap warning
Given two items overlapping only on a configured hotspot file, then the lint emits no overlap warning
Then the overlap audit never emits severity:'error' and never blocks finalize (overlap orders work, it does not refuse it)
Render the readiness report at pan plan finalize

narrative.Action: At pan plan finalize, render a human-readable readiness report from analyzeSwarmReadiness: dependency waves, conflict groups, and a file-overlap matrix, printed alongside the existing quality-lint output (src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts). Advisory only — it prints regardless of warnings and does not change the finalize pass/fail decision.

items.title: When pan plan finalize runs, then it prints a readiness report showing the dependency waves for the plan
Then the report renders the cross-item file-overlap matrix and any conflict groups, with hotspot files excluded
Then the report prints regardless of warnings and by itself neither blocks nor passes finalize (advisory)
Surface the readiness report in the VBriefViewer

narrative.Action: Surface the readiness report in the VBriefViewer (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/vbrief/): a panel rendering waves + overlap matrix + conflict groups from the same analyzer verdict. Expose the verdict to the frontend via the existing plan payload (the route serving GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan) or a sibling RPC — pick the lighter path at implementation. Follow pan-style-guide (no pill badges, no decorative color, muted status text).

items.title: Given a plan with a readiness verdict, when the VBriefViewer opens, then it renders a readiness panel showing the dependency waves
Then the panel renders the file-overlap matrix and conflict groups, with hotspot files excluded
Then the panel respects pan-style-guide (no pill badges, no decorative color, muted status text) and the frontend test suite passes
Record scope drift at pan done

narrative.Action: At pan done (src/cli/commands/done.ts) / the verification gate, compute the actual changed-file set vs origin/main (reuse changedFilesVsMain at src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts:107, which runs git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD) and diff it against the declared scope union (union of item files_scope from the spec). Record drift — files changed outside any declared scope, and declared scope files left untouched — into the workspace continue state and the review status (src/lib/review-status.ts) so it surfaces to the review role. RECORDING ONLY; the historical drift-table→confidence feedback loop (§10.8) is a deferred follow-up.

items.title: When pan done runs, then it computes the actual changed-file set vs origin/main and compares it to the declared scope union
Given files changed outside the declared scope union, then they are recorded as drift in the workspace continue state
Given declared scope files that were never touched, then they are recorded as drift
Then the drift record persists to the issue's review status so the review role's input contains it
Compute an issue's footprint from its declared scopes

narrative.Action: Add computeIssueFootprint(doc): string[] (pure deduped union of all item files_scope) to src/lib/vbrief/swarm-readiness.ts, plus a resolver that loads an issue's spec via findSpecByIssue (src/lib/pan-dir/specs.ts:216) and returns its footprint. This is the cross-issue signal input consumed by predicted-conflicts. Lands in the same module as readiness-analyzer (serialized after it).

items.title: Given a document with item file scopes, when computeIssueFootprint runs, then it returns the deduped union of all items' files_scope
Given an issue with no declared scopes, then computeIssueFootprint returns an empty array and does not throw
Given an on-main spec resolvable by findSpecByIssue, when the spec-resolving variant runs, then it returns that issue's footprint
Pre-branch predicted-conflict signal from declared footprints

expected_outputs: flywheel-merge-order.test.ts covers declared-footprint contribution and passes

narrative.Action: Extend the pairwise file-set conflict math in src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts (the conflict map at :197-214; planMergeTrain :66) so that issues WITHOUT a branch yet contribute their DECLARED footprint (computeIssueFootprint) to the pairwise predicted-conflict computation, converging to actual changedFilesVsMain (:107) once a branch exists. Exclude hotspot files. Surface the predicted-conflict counts on the merge-queue/backlog surface the flywheel consults — this IS the launch-ordering soft signal (soft preference, never a gate).

items.title: Given two not-yet-branched issues with overlapping declared footprints, when the conflict math runs, then their predicted-conflict count is non-zero
Given an issue that now has a branch, then its actual changedFilesVsMain set replaces its declared footprint in the computation
Given configured hotspot files, then they are excluded from the predicted-conflict computation
Then the predicted-conflict signal is advisory only — it changes ordering and never blocks a launch or a merge
Rebuild per-item registered slot spawning on spawnRun

expected_outputs: spawnRun accepts slot options and registers agent-<issue>-<n>

narrative.Action: Re-add per-item registered slot spawning to spawnRun (src/lib/agents/spawn.ts:72), reversing the PAN-1517 removal (spawn-prep.ts:83-84; 'one work agent per issue, period' spawn.ts:365). Re-introduce OPTIONAL SpawnRunOptions slot fields (slotIndex, slotItemId); create a per-item worktree on branch feature/<issue>/slot-<n>; register the agent as agent-<issue>-<n> with its own state file (existing dashboard resource-discovery already nests it under the issue — no new UI). Respect a configurable cap on concurrent registered slots (hazard H3). Teardown compat already exists (kill.ts:49). Keep the existing single-work-agent path the DEFAULT — slot spawning is opt-in via the new options.

items.title: Given slot options (slotIndex, slotItemId), when spawnRun runs, then it creates a worktree on feature/<issue>/slot-<n> and registers an agent named agent-<issue>-<n> with its own state file
Given no slot options, when spawnRun runs, then it preserves the current one-work-agent-per-issue behavior unchanged
Then resource-discovery surfaces agent-<issue>-<n> nested under its issue (no new dialog) and cost is attributed to that agent id
Given a configured maximum number of concurrent registered slots, when more slots are requested, then spawns beyond the cap wait or are refused (the cap is respected)
When pan kill <issue> runs, then it tears down slot agents and their worktrees via the existing agent-<issue>-<n> recognition
Pure dispatch-tier decision (the PAN-1791 tier-table seam)

narrative.Action: Create a pure chooseDispatchTier(item): 'in-context' | 'registered-slot' in a dedicated module src/lib/agents/dispatch-tier.ts, deciding ONLY from item metadata (difficulty, files_scope, readiness, files_scope_confidence): cheap/mechanical + high-confidence → in-context; heavy (complex|expert) or anything to distribute → registered-slot; complex|expert default to foreman-serialized unless explicitly parallel-safe with a stated reason (§10.2). It MUST be a pure function of metadata so PAN-1791's per-difficulty tier table can drive it WITHOUT a second dispatcher. No PAN-1791-specific (or any issue-id-specific) branches.

items.title: Given a low-difficulty, high-confidence, non-distributed item, when chooseDispatchTier runs, then it returns 'in-context'
Given a complex or expert item, when chooseDispatchTier runs, then it returns 'registered-slot'
Given identical item metadata, then chooseDispatchTier returns the identical tier every time (pure — no I/O, no global state)
Then no code path special-cases a specific issue id; grep over the module confirms the decision reads only difficulty/files_scope/readiness metadata
Per-slot verify-then-merge into the feature branch

expected_outputs: verifyAndMergeSlot refuses merge on red verify_commands
clean merge into feature branch on green

narrative.Action: Create verifyAndMergeSlot(issue, slotIndex, item) in src/lib/agents/slot-merge.ts that, in the slot worktree: (1) runs the item's verify_commands and asserts its expected_outputs are present; (2) ONLY on green, merges feature/<issue>/slot-<n> into the feature branch with a clean (or foreman-resolved) 3-way merge; (3) returns a terse structured result (verified, merged, conflicts, evidence). On red it does NOT merge and returns the failure for the foreman's redo-then-serial protocol. This fixes the old swarm's biggest gap (slots merged with zero gates) and keeps the feature branch continuously green. Use async exec/spawn — never execSync.

items.title: Given an item whose verify_commands fail in the slot worktree, when verifyAndMergeSlot runs, then it does not merge the slot branch and returns a failure result
Given green verify_commands and present expected_outputs, when verifyAndMergeSlot runs, then it merges the slot branch into the feature branch
Given a non-overlapping slot branch, then a clean 3-way merge succeeds; given a conflicting slot branch, then the conflict is surfaced (not force-applied) for foreman resolution
Given a successful slot merge, then the feature branch contains the slot's committed changes
Reconcile slot state so a respawned foreman resumes mid-swarm

narrative.Action: Create a reconcile helper in src/lib/agents/slot-reconcile.ts that the foreman calls on (re)start: enumerate existing slot branches (feature/<issue>/slot-*) and registered slot agents (agent-<issue>-<n>), cross-reference the spec's statusOverrides, and return the wave state (which items are merged / in-flight / pending) so a respawned foreman resumes the correct wave instead of restarting the swarm (hazard H2).

items.title: Given existing feature/<issue>/slot-* branches and statusOverrides, when reconcile runs, then it returns which items are already merged, in-flight, or pending
Given a slot branch already contained in the feature branch, then reconcile reports that item complete and it is not re-dispatched
Given no slot branches present, then reconcile returns a clean initial state (nothing in flight)
Foreman wave-driver protocol in roles/work.md

narrative.Action: Replace/extend the 'Parallel work via subagents' section of roles/work.md (:70) with the foreman protocol: (1) read the DAG and compute waves (groupItemsByWave); (2) consult analyzeSwarmReadiness for swarmability + intra-wave serialization (overlap orders, never blocks); (3) per item, chooseDispatchTier → in-context subagent OR registered slot (slot-spawn-tier); (4) on item completion, verifyAndMergeSlot before the slot merges into the feature branch; (5) at DAG convergence points (item with >1 blocking parents), compose an in-context synthesis digest from the merged parents' expected_outputs and inline it into the convergent item's dispatch prompt (no SynthesisOutput state); (6) failure handling: one redo with feedback, then serial fallback in the foreman's own context; (7) on (re)start, run the reconcile helper to resume mid-swarm. Keep the existing serial per-bead workflow as the DEFAULT; the foreman path is for swarm-eligible plans.

items.title: Then roles/work.md documents the foreman wave loop: waves via groupItemsByWave, swarmability/serialization via analyzeSwarmReadiness, and per-item tier via chooseDispatchTier
Then roles/work.md instructs the foreman to verify-then-merge each slot (verify_commands green + expected_outputs present) before it merges into the feature branch
Then roles/work.md specifies composing an in-context synthesis digest from merged parents' expected_outputs at convergence points, with no resurrected runtime state
Then roles/work.md specifies the failure protocol (one redo with feedback, then serial fallback) and the reconcile-on-restart step
End-to-end: foreman dispatches a real multi-item plan (dogfood)

expected_outputs: foreman-swarm.test.ts exercises dispatch+verify+merge of a multi-item plan and passes

narrative.Action: Add an end-to-end test/dogfood (tests/integration/foreman-swarm.test.ts, taggable @slow if it spawns real registered agents) exercising the full foreman path on a REAL multi-item plan whose items pass the readiness lint as swarm-eligible (independent vertical slices with declared files_scope + verify_commands). Candidate plan: PAN-1791's buildable WI-1 (tier-table config) + WI-2 (plan-time scheduler/relevance-map) slice IF swarmable at the time; otherwise a representative synthetic multi-item plan. Operator ask #1. The dispatch path MUST contain no PAN-1791-specific code — swarmability is a plan property certified by the readiness analyzer.

items.title: Given a multi-item plan the readiness analyzer certifies swarm-eligible, when the foreman runs, then it dispatches the non-overlapping wave as registered slot agents, each in its own worktree on feature/<issue>/slot-<n>
Given each dispatched slot, when it completes, then it merges into the feature branch only on green verify_commands, and the final feature branch contains all items
Then the end-to-end path selects dispatch tiers purely from item metadata — a grep over the dispatch path confirms no issue-id special-casing for PAN-1791
Then resource-discovery reports the dispatched slot agents nested under the issue during the run
